payload.title: Finance Committee Meeting
payload.description: Finance Committee Meeting book for February 18, 2021 containing agenda items, staff summaries, financial reports, and action items including CBDTP bond authorization, 2020 TBTA operating surplus, MRT escalator payments, LIRR procurement, fare evasion reports, and real estate agenda items.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority
tool_args.raw_text: Metropolitan Transportation Authority - Finance Committee Meeting February 2021

tool_args.raw_text: New York City Transit Authority - MTA New York City Transit

tool_args.raw_text: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority (TBTA or MTA Bridges and Tunnels)

tool_args.label: MTA Bus Company
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Bus Company - MTA Bus Company

MTA Finance Department is seeking TBTA Board authorization for issuance of TBTA CBDTP Second Subordinate Revenue Obligations in an amount not to exceed $506 million to fund costs related to the design, build, and installation of infrastructure, tolling systems and allowable implementation expenses necessary for the operation of the Central Business District Tolling Program

payload.description: MTA Finance Department seeking TBTA Board authorization for issuance of up to $506 million in bonds/notes to fund design, build, and installation of infrastructure, tolling systems and allowable implementation expenses for CBDTP

tool_args.raw_text: Thursday, 2/18/2021, 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM ET, 2 Broadway, 20th Floor Board Room, New York, NY 10004

payload.description: MTA Finance Committee and Board meeting to consider, among other items, authorization of CBDTP bond issuance up to $506 million, TBTA 2020 operating surplus transfer, MRT escalator payments, and LIRR train simulator procurement

tool_args.raw_text: TRIBOROUGH BRIDGE AND TUNNEL AUTHORITY MULTIPLE SERIES 2021 BOND ANTICIPATION NOTES AND RELATED SUBORDINATED INDEBTEDNESS CBDTP SECOND SUBORDINATE REVENUE BOND SUPPLEMENTAL RESOLUTION Adopted February 18, 2021

payload.description: TBTA Board adoption of Multiple Series 2021 Bond Anticipation Notes and Related Subordinated Indebtedness CBDTP Second Subordinate Revenue Bond Supplemental Resolution authorizing up to $506 million in notes and bonds for CBDTP

tool_args.raw_text: LIRR requests MTA Board approval to award a competitively negotiated contract in the amount of $3,186,584 to Corys, Inc. to design, manufacture, test and deliver Train Simulator Systems for each of its M-7/M-9, Dual Mode (DM) Locomotive and C-3 fleets.

payload.project_name: LIRR M-7/M-9, DM Locomotive, C3 Train Simulator Systems
payload.description: LIRR competitively awarded contract to Corys, Inc. for $3,186,584 to design, manufacture, test and deliver Train Simulator Systems for M-7/M-9, DM Locomotive and C-3 fleets to support training for East Side Access and Positive Train Control

warnings: project.partner_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Partner agency is relation context. Suggested relation: has_partner.

tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North is investing in infrastructure improvements at the Hartsdale Station to support ADA accessibility and enhance pedestrian flow. The Project includes 2 new elevators, mechanical rooms, expanded sidewalks and new curbing, for both the inbound and outbound sides of the station with connections to the existing pedestrian overpass.

payload.description: MTA Metro-North Railroad acquisition of permanent and temporary easements from Hartsdale Public Parking District for ADA improvements including 2 new elevators, mechanical rooms, expanded sidewalks and new curbing at Hartsdale Station
payload.location: Hartsdale, Town of Greenburgh, New York

location_normalized.raw_text: Hartsdale, Town of Greenburgh, New York

tool_args.raw_text: Farebox Revenue $90.1 $170.2 $80.1 88.9 - Passenger revenue higher at NYCT, MTA Bus, LIRR, and MNR

payload.description: Farebox revenue favorable variance - passenger revenue higher at NYCT, MTA Bus, LIRR, and MNR

tool_args.raw_text: Total All Agencies Year-to-Date Through December: 2020 953,188,845 -62.72% vs 2019

tool_args.raw_text: The amount of surplus available for transfer to the MTA and NYCTA is $472,754,532. The amount of investment income that is surplus funds and available to transfer to MTA for fiscal year 2020 is $763,475.

payload.description: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority operating surplus for fiscal year 2020 available for transfer to MTA and NYCTA

tool_args.raw_text: no fare was collected on local bus service for most of the April-September period, rendering fare evasion a moot issue.

payload.claim_text: No fare was collected on local bus service for most of the April-September 2020 period, rendering fare evasion a moot issue.
payload.description: Bus fare evasion data collection gap due to pandemic fare-free period

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

tool_args.raw_text: new report is based on all data collected using Automatic Passenger Counters (APCs). APCs have been installed on 37% of non-SBS local buses which is sufficient to provide robust data for 84% of non-SBS local routes

payload.claim_text: APCs have been installed on 37% of non-SBS local buses which is sufficient to provide robust data for 84% of non-SBS local routes
payload.description: Bus fare evasion now based on Automatic Passenger Counter (APC) data instead of on-board staff surveys

tool_args.raw_text: On January 26, 2021, MTA executed a 2,826,779 gallon ultra-low sulfur diesel fuel hedge with J.P. Morgan Ventures Energy Corporation at an all-in price of $1.6051/gallon.

payload.description: Ultra-low sulfur diesel fuel hedge executed on January 26, 2021 with J.P. Morgan Ventures Energy Corporation

tool_args.raw_text: at an all-in price of $1.6051/gallon

payload.description: All-in price for ultra-low sulfur diesel fuel hedge
